seg: [music] hi there. my name is cameron simmons. i'm an applications engineer here at

hybrid voltage current pulser referred to as a precision voltage pulser. um, and it's got a lot of cool features that may help your application and we we're
going to show those off. so, enjoy. so, the pdx 25506 is what we refer to as a precision pulse generator. uh, because we tune the overshoot and undershoot so
that it never exceeds the pulse top and bottom voltage. and this can be really good for semiconductor material testing because you know your material is never
exposed to a voltage higher than required for your testing. get really good deal. the unit can actually do 50 volts at 10 amps uh which is extremely
high for a voltage pulser of this type. um can really test a lot of high voltage and high current semiconductor materials
this way. and another great feature is actually it accepts a high voltage power supply and a lower voltage power supply so that you can actually float it up
to 10 volts. um, and again, this can be really good if that's the voltages that you want to test it at. um, or if you just want a less noisy signal moving
above ground like that, take out some of the noise and help you get cleaner data. so, here i'll show you the cable that's included with the 256. as you can
see, it's nice heavy duty connector with gold pads, very low inductance. same thing with this cable. it can take a lot of voltage, but it's also very low
inductance, so you're not going to get any added overshoot, no ringing. overall, very, very good cable for this application. here's kind of an
interesting feature about the 256. has sort of a utility section here that uh will kind of help you invert and copy your trigger signal. so you can take one
trigger in from your source uh and you can instead put it in the rf logic input and then as you can see you'll get uh a ttl copy of that signal uh and an
inverted ttl version of this signal. um and this will trigger off rf as well. so you can put a sinosoidal waveform in and
it will create a square trigger signal. um, and then say you wanted to trigger your oscilloscope to this one to see uh other aspects of the signal and then you
could take the inverted rf out and then have that be your actual trigger signal for the unit itself for the output. um, and then you can switch those two

no overshoots, very square pulse, low noise as well. uh then we'll go ahead and zoom in on the rising edge and you can see the overshoot tuning at work. so

seg: in today's video we will be modifying a pvx 4151 high voltage pulse generator so that its output can be biased up 1,000 volts the pvx 4151 is a bipolar pulse
generator so it has two high voltage inputs for positive and negative polarity the power supply cannot exceed 1500 volts differential but they can be
asymmetrical about zero for example -500 vol to 1,000 vol -750 vol to 750 vol 0 to 1500 volts etc what if we wanted to stay within the 1500 volt differential
limit but bias it up so it pulses from 1,000 volts to 2500 volts this is actually possible with a small modification there are many possible
applications for a dc biased high voltage pulse output signal one example might be parallel plates in a time of flight mass spectrometer an experiment
might require that both plates be held at a high voltage while one is pulsed above the other one in order to achieve a certain velocity if you know of any
other applications for a signal like this please leave it in the comments in order to do this we'll need two positive high voltage power supplies this lower
5kb supply is going to act as the bias voltage the problem here is that most high voltage power supplies can't actually regulate voltages of the same
polarity at different levels basically the higher voltage pulse toop power supply is going to try to pull the lower voltage up to its same potential and to
prevent this what we have to do is provide the bi supply with a separate path to ground and that's going to be through a shunt resistor that will allow
it to regulate the voltage but limit the current the most crucial part of this is selecting the correct resistance so it
needs to be able to have the right resistance to provide the right amount of regulation to maintain the correct differential voltage and then it also
needs to be enough have enough power handleing to not melt with from the power dissipated in it and we can show those equations
here the resistance of the shunt will be determined by the bias voltage divided by the current from the power supply the
power supply current itself can be extrapolated from the equation for power draw in a capacitive load here we see that below power equals c the internal
capacitance cable capacitance and load capacitance combined multiplied by the output voltage squared multiplied by the output frequency then we can plug that
in to the power divided by the bias voltage and this will give us the current drawn from the power supply in our experiment we will be pulsing
between 1,000 volt and 2450 volt and our load is 50 pico farad this capacitor is connected with 2 ft of rg58 cable px 4150 51 it has an internal capacitance
of 200 pof farad so for my specific setup i'm going to be using a 62 mega ohm resistor from the negative supply to gri what i did is basically soldered
together a few 10 mega ohm 1 watt resistors the current should be in the microamp so we're not going to exceed any wat limits here i put spade
connectors on either side so that it can be removed easily i also wrapped it in this captain tape which is going to isolate the voltage and reason for that
is you know we are technically exceeding the voltage limit the 4150 by biasing it up and so the case isn't really designed
for higher differential voltages i don't think it should be a problem but better sa sorry with our shun resistor in place
we can now enable the power supplies and slowly start turning up the voltage on our hindinger evo this is the higher voltage supply you'll see on the bias
supply it will start showing output voltage regardless of the fact that we have not turned it up this means our shunt is working

seg: foreign [music] the best neutron detector on the market today we will be discussing the unique
properties of helium-3 that make it an ideal choice for neutron detection firstly helium-3 is highly efficient when it comes to detecting neutrons
when a neutron enters the helium-3 detector it is absorbed by the helium-3 nucleus and this results in the formation of a triton and a proton so
these particles are detected providing very clear signal of the neutrons presence even three detectors are very sensitive
and they can detect almost all the neutrons that enter them secondly helium-3 detectors have a low background noise this is because helium-3 has a low
natural radiant activity making its background signal low compared to other neutron detectors so this makes helium 3 detectors ideal for use and applications
for the background signal is a significant concern such as nuclear nonproliferation and safeguards thirdly helium-3 detectors can operate at low
temperatures the sensitivity of helium-3 to neutrons is enhanced at low temperatures making it ideal for use in applications where low noise and high
sensitivity are critical such as in nuclear physics experiments and research however there are also some drawbacks to using
helium-3 detectors for one helium-3 is a rare isotope and it's expensive to produce so this raises concerns about how long-term availability for use in
neutron detection will be additionally helium-3 detectors are also sensitive to cosmic rays which can cause false positives or incorrect readings
uh limiting they're used in some applications such as in aviation security but in recent years resource researchers have been exploring alternative neutron
detectors to replace helium 3 detectors but one of the most promising at the time right now is boron 10 detectors which has a similar efficiency to
helium-3 and can be produced at lower cost however for our time detectors do have a higher background noise compared to helium 3
detectors and do require a higher voltage to operate which can limit their use in various applications so that's all we have for today

seg: foreign [music] detection and measurement there are several types of detectors that are used quite common let's take a look at the
top five first we have helium-3 this detector uses a principle of ionization to detect neutrons when a neutron collides with the nucleus of a
helium-3 atom it creates a charged particle which can then be detected and measured this type of detector is highly sensitive and is often used in
applications like nuclear power plants where accurate neutron measurements are critical next we have the boron trifluoride
this detector also uses ionization to detect neutrons but instead of using helium-3 it uses boron trifluoride gas as the tech as the detecting medium the
boron 10 isotope present in the gas is a good neutron absorber and a charged particle is created which can be detected and measured this detector also
is highly sensitive and is commonly used in nuclear facilities as well as research and development of nuclear technology
third we have the lithium 6 stimulation detector this detector uses the principle of scintillation where a neutron collides with the lithium-6
nucleus causing it to emit light which can then be detected and measured this type of detector is highly sensitive and is often used in applications such as
neutron radiography and imaging where resolution is required fourth we have the boron line detector this detector is similar to the boron
trifluoride proportional counter but instead of using the boron trifluoride gas it uses a bore online detector tube detector is also highly sensitive and is
often used in similar applications as the boron trifluoride proportional counter lastly we have the domino tile neutron detector so this is a device
that uses utilizes micro constructor semiconductor neutron detector technology to detect thermal neutrons the device uses a technology called msnd
that converts lithium-6 to thermal neutrons with the an efficiency of about 30 percent it is designed to be thin and flexible
with an adjustable detection area with low power consumption but additionally it also has digital outputs capabilities that can be detected in a tile format
for greater coverage more recently a similar reaction with lithium-6 is used for neutron detection such as click and clbc detectors these
detectors are used quite often and they offer both gamma spectroscopy and neutron detection so that's always good these detectors yield good gamma

videos.title: Approaches in RF Power Measurement | BNC
videos.author: Berkeley Nucleonics Media
seg: foreign [music] power measured well to start high frequency diodes detect the rf voltage
developed across a terminating load resistor the diodes directly perform an ac to dc conversion and the dc voltage is measured by the power meter and
scaled to produce a power readout the relation to the dc voltage to the power measured is dependent on the diode region of operation which is typically
separated into three sections the square law region occurs at levels below about negative 20 dbm where the diodes produce
a dc voltage output that is closely proportional to the square of the applied rf voltage this region is also modulation independent the linear region
is found at levels above zero dbm where the dc voltage output is proportional to the peak rf voltage the transition region of a diode sensor is at levels
between negative 20 dbm and 0 dbm diode behavior must be meticulously characterized within this region to produce accurate power measurements
peak power sensors use a low impedance load across the smoothing capacitors to discharge them very quickly when the rf amplitude drops
this in combination with a very small smoothing capacitance allows peak power sensors to achieve fast rise times and wide video bandwidths average power
sensors condition the input rf voltage which force diodes to operate in their square law region this enables them to accurately measure average power of
signals virtually regardless of modulation foreign [music]
